articles.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems ‘in every major operating system and web browser’
articles.summary: Anthropic launches Project Glasswing, partnering with tech giants to use AI model Claude Mythos Preview for automated cybersecurity vulnerability detection across operating systems and browsers.

articles.title: Suno and major music labels reportedly clash over AI music sharing
articles.summary: Suno AI music maker and major labels Universal Music Group and Sony Music clash over licensing deals, disagreeing on whether users can share AI-generated songs freely online.

articles.title: Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
articles.summary: Intel is partnering with Elon Musk to design and build the Terafab AI chip factory in Austin, Texas. The facility will supply AI chips for Musk's SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI ventures including self-driving cars and humanoid robots.

articles.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
articles.summary: Firmus, an Nvidia-backed AI data center builder, reached a $5.5B valuation after raising $1.35B in six months, positioning itself as a major infrastructure provider for Asia's growing AI demand.

articles.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project
articles.summary: Intel joins SpaceX and Tesla's Terafab project to build a U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, though Intel's specific role remains undefined.

articles.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
articles.summary: Anthropic previews Mythos, a powerful AI model designed for defensive cybersecurity work. The model will be tested by select high-profile companies in a new security initiative.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The investment targets developer frustration with legacy cloud complexity and costs.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, prompting interest in Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block offering similar autonomous coding capabilities without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens that led to a coding challenge, attracting thousands of applicants and helping the startup recruit engineers.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks due to its broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such powerful permissions to autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: New Rowhammer attacks exploit memory bit flips in Nvidia GPUs to give malicious users complete root control of shared cloud machines. The vulnerability targets high-performance GPU cards frequently used in multi-tenant environments.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent research shows quantum computers require far fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption. Neutral atom and Google approaches could crack 256-bit cryptography in under 10 days with 100x less overhead.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Middle East desalination plants face increasing vulnerability amid regional conflicts. Iran accused the US of attacking a facility, disrupting water supplies to multiple villages, highlighting critical infrastructure risks.

articles.title: Enabling agent-first process redesign
articles.summary: AI agents can dynamically learn and optimize workflows autonomously, but companies must redesign processes around agents rather than retrofitting them into legacy systems.

articles.title: The Download: AI’s impact on jobs, and data centres in space
articles.summary: Economists increasingly acknowledge AI's potential job displacement threat. Researcher Alex Imas argues collecting price elasticity data is crucial for addressing AI's economic impact.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run on-device. The model combines advanced intelligence capabilities with local processing for enhanced privacy and efficiency.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use technology, advancing AI capabilities for human-computer interaction and automation.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI visual understanding capabilities. The system demonstrates improved accuracy in analyzing and interpreting complex visual data for real-world applications.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for sustainable farming innovation.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new Gemini API inference tiers—Flex and Priority—to help developers balance costs and latency in their applications.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technologies, enabling high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to monitor and protect Brazil's forests from deforestation.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source AI models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with better precision and lower latency, enabling more natural and fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI's potential for harmful manipulation in finance and health sectors, developing new safety measures to protect people.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while the podcast discusses recent AI developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate enhanced visual details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math, science, and data visualization.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to deploy physical AI robots for enterprise perimeter security, combining autonomous patrols with AI analytics to enable real-time incident response and reduce security response times.

articles.title: Boomi calls it “data activation” and says it’s the missing step in every AI deployment
articles.summary: Enterprise AI deployments fail due to fragmented, inconsistently labeled data spread across disconnected applications. Boomi identifies this 'data activation' problem as critical to solve before AI agents can function effectively.

articles.title: Anthropic’s refusal to arm AI is exactly why the UK wants it
articles.summary: Anthropic refused US Pentagon demands to remove AI safety guardrails for autonomous weapons and surveillance, prompting Trump to ban federal use of the company. The UK subsequently expanded Anthropic operations, valuing its ethical stance.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed automated methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by answering localization, destination, and pathfinding questions.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI models.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases EUPE: A Compact Vision Encoder Family Under 100M Parameters That Rivals Specialist Models Across Image Understanding, Dense Prediction, and VLM Tasks
articles.summary: Meta AI released EUPE, a compact vision encoder under 100M parameters that efficiently handles multiple vision tasks simultaneously without sacrificing performance, addressing the trade-off between model size and capability.

articles.title: An Implementation Guide to Running NVIDIA Transformer Engine with Mixed Precision, FP8 Checks, Benchmarking, and Fallback Execution
articles.summary: Tutorial implementing NVIDIA Transformer Engine with mixed-precision (FP8) acceleration in Python, comparing baseline PyTorch against optimized paths with benchmarking and performance analysis.

articles.title: RightNow AI Releases AutoKernel: An Open-Source Framework that Applies an Autonomous Agent Loop to GPU Kernel Optimization for Arbitrary PyTorch Models
articles.summary: RightNow AI released AutoKernel, an open-source framework using autonomous LLM agents to automatically optimize GPU kernels for PyTorch models, eliminating the need for GPU expertise.

articles.title: LG C6 vs. LG C5: I compared two generations of OLED TVs, and it was pretty darn close
articles.summary: LG C6 is a refreshed flagship OLED TV that closely compares to the C5 model, raising questions about whether upgrading between these two generations is worthwhile for consumers.

articles.title: I swapped my MacBook Air with a Snapdragon X Elite Extreme laptop - here's how they compared
articles.summary: An Asus Zenbook A16 with Snapdragon X Elite Extreme processor was compared to a MacBook Air. The ultraportable laptop offers a fresh approach but performance depends on individual needs.

articles.title: Google Chrome's vertical tabs are here: How to opt in and use the new Reading Mode
articles.summary: Google Chrome now officially supports vertical tabs, joining other browsers in this feature. Users can opt in to try the new Reading Mode and organize tabs vertically.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, causing corrupted or nonsensical outputs. The article addresses managing these conflicts in multi-agent orchestration systems.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: The article discusses top 5 reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system performance by improving result relevance and accuracy.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards to more visible applications. Key trends to watch in 2026 include emerging developments in AI deployment and integration.

articles.title: Democratizing Marketing Mix Models (MMM) with Open Source and Gen AI
articles.summary: A system combining open-source Bayesian marketing mix models with GenAI enables transparent, vendor-independent marketing analytics insights, making advanced analytics more accessible.

articles.title: From 4 Weeks to 45 Minutes: Designing a Document Extraction System for 4,700+ PDFs
articles.summary: A hybrid PyMuPDF and GPT-4 Vision pipeline reduced PDF document extraction from 4 weeks to 45 minutes, replacing £8,000 in manual work while demonstrating that latest models aren't always necessary.

articles.title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Deep Dive
articles.summary: This article explores context engineering techniques for optimizing how AI agents utilize their limited context window resources effectively.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local, on-device AI execution across various systems from RTX PCs to data centers. The small, efficient models enable agentic AI with real-time context without relying on cloud infrastructure.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy title.

articles.title: Building real-time conversational podcasts with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables rapid podcast production with real-time conversational AI, reducing traditional production time and costs while supporting multiple languages and seamless voice-text interaction.

articles.title: Text-to-SQL solution powered by Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock's text-to-SQL solution enables business users to query databases in natural language, reducing bottlenecks and freeing technical teams for complex work.

articles.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick enables HR teams to build no-code AI agents that automate employee onboarding tasks like answering questions and tracking compliance, reducing manual work and accelerating new hire productivity.

articles.title: Running AI Workloads on Rack-Scale Supercomputers: From Hardware to Topology-Aware Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 supercomputers use Blackwell architecture with 18 compute trays and high-bandwidth networking for efficient AI workload execution. Topology-aware scheduling optimizes performance across rack-scale systems.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 accelerates vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling to match improved model throughput using CUDA acceleration.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launch, designed to scale from NVIDIA Blackwell data centers to edge Jetson devices for diverse deployment needs.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations view social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology by combining diverse skills and perspectives outside traditional tech paths.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand how these complex systems make decisions, advancing AI transparency and safety.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven design methods for imaging systems that optimize how well noisy measurements distinguish between objects using encoders and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
